title: 8 AI Cost Optimization Strategies for Product & Engineering Leaders

sections.title: AI Cost Problem Overview
key_points: 90% of CIOs say cost limits their ability to get value from AI
Most AI spend comes from model usage, GPU utilization, and inefficient workflows
Companies can reduce LLM spend by 30-60% with proper optimization
Cost overruns from overusing large models for simple tasks and poor prompt design

sections.title: AI Pricing Models
key_points: Token-based pricing: GPT-5 ~$1.25 input/$10 output per 1M tokens
Bedrock pricing varies: Google Gemma 3 ~$0.00004 per 1K input tokens
Infrastructure costs: tens of dollars per hour per GPU unit
One million tokens ≈ 750,000 words of text

sections.title: Training Costs
key_points: Smaller models: $50K-$1M for few billion parameters
Mid-sized models: $1M-$6M for ~70B parameters
Frontier models: GPT-3 cost $3M-$7M, GPT-4 estimated $41M-$78M
Largest models can cost up to $200M+ for training

sections.title: 8 Cost Optimization Strategies
key_points: 1. Route requests across models intelligently based on complexity
2. Design token-efficient prompts as versioned assets
3. Batch inference for non-interactive workloads
4. Optimize retrieval depth in RAG pipelines
5. Apply quantization and distillation strategically
6. Align infrastructure scaling with usage patterns
7. Instrument cost at feature and workflow level
8. Establish continuous AI FinOps practices

sections.title: Implementation Best Practices
key_points: Use lightweight models for routine tasks, advanced models selectively
Standardize prompt templates and output schemas
Focus on retrieval precision over recall in RAG systems
Track cost per feature for better product decisions
Combine autoscaling with pre-warming for critical paths

summary.title: Comprehensive AI Cost Optimization Guide for Production Systems
takeaways: AI cost management is critical for production systems with 90% of CIOs citing cost as a barrier to AI value
Training costs range from $50K for small models to $200M+ for frontier models, but most enterprises benefit more from fine-tuning
Token-based pricing varies dramatically: GPT-5 costs $10 per 1M output tokens vs Google Gemma 3 at $0.00008 per 1K tokens
Eight proven strategies can reduce LLM spend by 30-60% without sacrificing performance
Model routing, prompt optimization, and RAG tuning are the highest-impact cost reduction techniques
concepts: Model routing and intelligent request distribution
actionable: Implement a routing layer to use lightweight models for simple tasks and advanced models only when needed
Standardize prompt templates with explicit output schemas and strict context boundaries
Batch non-interactive workloads to increase GPU utilization and reduce per-request overhead
Optimize RAG systems by focusing on retrieval precision and implementing query-aware caching
Track cost at the feature and workflow level rather than aggregate spend for better decision-making
Establish regular AI FinOps reviews with shared dashboards and budget guardrails
Use autoscaling policies tuned specifically for AI workload patterns
Apply quantization and distillation strategically to high-volume endpoints after validation
